message:
Security & UX Fixes: Windows command injection and URL punctuation

- Fix critical security vulnerability in Windows browser launcher where unsanitized URLs could trigger command injection via 'cmd /c start'. Switched to 'rundll32 url.dll,FileProtocolHandler'.
- Fix UX issue where detected URLs often included trailing punctuation (like '.', ')', or ','), breaking the link when opened. Added 'cleanURL' helper to trim common punctuation.
